author: ben_a_adams
content: Is just treating them like normal tx; can only include so many blob tx etc

author: ben_a_adams
content: If it makes deposit count too high, skip, like you would if it made block gas too high or blobs too high

author: ben_a_adams
content: Efficient building is probably harder; and validating messy

author: potuz
content: The problem of not making this part of consensus is that we can still be dossed by a malicious builder, we are counting arlready on builders to not include this many deposits, the issue is how do we harden the CL to either handle these or limit these as part of consensus.

author: barnabasbusa
content: how do we get dosed by malicious builder tho?

author: barnabasbusa
content: if we limit to 512 deposits per slot and have 8000 epoch of dequeue it’s gonna be expensive

author: barnabasbusa
content: like 0.5B expensive

author: barnabasbusa
content: and honestly if we do see someone attacking this way we can just increase it to 1024 deposit

author: barnabasbusa
content: ~~and should be able to do this without a fork~~

author: potuz
content: limiting works with an extra time to dequeue so that there's a high cost to produce this attack.

author: potuz
content: but I thought limiting as part of consensus is hard on the EL, notice that Ben's PR is not about consensus, it's just limiting in the miner

author: potuz
content: Also kinda weird that this can't be exploited as a DOS on the miner, if I generate in an intricate way a transaction that only after spending millions in gas triggers a deposit and now you need to take it out of the block

author: potuz
content: I do not know how <@316280621783580673> is protecting from that

author: ben_a_adams
content:
CL fails to import block; can it default back to local built or is that not possible in ePBS

Idea on changing our building was so didn't have a round robin of every EL overbuilding if no one else does it

author: ben_a_adams
content: So at least the chain makes progress

author: potuz
content: We could make it a CL failure on such blocks as we talked in ACD, and then pray that the EL can deal with this type of DOS in itself, but this starts to feel incredibly brittle: we patch a DOS by introducing another

author: ben_a_adams
content: As long as chain can make progress builders would be disincentivised to build blocks that will be rejected by CL? (Much like building a block that uses too much gas)

author: barnabasbusa
content: could the el not fork away if an “invalid” block is created that has over x deposits ?

author: ben_a_adams
content: If the chain can't make progress than is issue?

author: potuz
content: yes but the DOS is that builders can only find out that the tx will invalidate their block **after** simulating it

author: potuz
content: and this could cost a lot of work just to drop the entire thing without any cost

author: potuz
content: again, it's not a problem to cap it and declare the block invalid, the problem is how to cap it without making it a DOS on the EL

author: ben_a_adams
content: 🤔  is that not what we do now? Run the tx, reverse out if bad, add new tx

author: potuz
content: what? now I am puzzled, I thought pre AA you do not have this problem with arbitrary execution, when you start simulating you know the cost will be paid

author: potuz
content: if the transaction reverts you can still put it in

author: potuz
content: with AA this becomes harder but still the validation frame is constrained

author: ben_a_adams
content: yeah but you don't know what tip will be till you run it

author: ben_a_adams
content: as is actual_gas * tip; not gas_limit * tip

author: potuz
content: that's fine, that is very different than allowing a tx with the maximum gas limit to execute and only invalidating it at the end, no one pays and you move to the next high paying tx, which can be from the same attacker

author: ben_a_adams
content: true

author: potuz
content: and the attacker is safe cause you cannot include these txs at all

author: ben_a_adams
content: GAS2ETH (charge portion of total blockgas) + new deposit contract 😅 https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-7791
